title: Children's Clothing - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Children's Clothing 36 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Foster Children 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides basic needs, essential support services, crisis intervention, and advocacy. Services include a drop-in center, food pantry, children's clothing, school supplies, personal grooming supplies, feminine hygiene supplies, mentoring, information and referrals for other assistance agencies, and assistance in applying for identification. 817 S 10th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 and their families who are experiencing homelessness Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 1pm-5pm Summer: Tuesday-Wednesday 1pm-5pm (541) 267-3104 7115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BEAUTIFUL SAVIOR LUTHERAN CHURCH CLOTHING BOUTIQUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers recycled clothing for people of all ages, including items suitable for work, interviews, and school. Appointments are limited to one per month. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday 9am-noon. Additional hours available for those fostering a child. Email (503) 788-7000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BABY RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provide material goods such as baby diapers, wipes, formula, children clothing from new born to age 8. 207 NE Park Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Families Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 426-9411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES EASTERN OREGON BABY RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provide material goods such as baby diapers, wipes, formula, children clothing from new born to age 8. 2725 7th Street Room 002 Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Families Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm (541) 524-2331 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DALLAS EVANGELICAL CHURCH COMMUNITY CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, towels and linens. Donations accepted. 783 SW Church Street Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9am-noon, Thursday 7pm-9pm, Friday-Saturday 9am-noon, as long as approved volunteer leaders are available Email (503) 623-3778 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator tool with list of Family Resource Centers (FRCs) by school district. Provides a range of services and support to families with children, including child literacy groups and story times, playgroups, assistance with accessing school personnel, parent education, parent literacy and computer support, and other drop-in services for families and children. Some locations may offer children's clothing and other supplies. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email (541) 741-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELYSIUM SANCTUARY FAMILY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, shoes, school supplies, and birthday party supplies for foster families, and shares information about community events and activities through published or online calendars Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Resource (Foster) families Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER ITEMS EXCHANGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people donating furniture and appliances to those needing those items. Small items like blankets and hygiene items can be brought to the Family Resource Center. 3520 Frontage Road Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (541) 271-9700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF PRINEVILLE HOPE'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers clothing, linen, shoes, and grooming supplies. 450 SE Fairview Street Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Foster and adoptive children 0-18 years old Hours: 1st, 2nd, 3th and 5th Thursdays of the month 9am-11am; 4th Thursday 12:30pm-2:30pm Email (541) 848-0138 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF LEBANON BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, utility assistance, heating fuel assistance, prescription assistance, and clothing. Also offers financial assistance to obtain a valid ID card or driver's license through the Oregon Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV). Assistance is available once per year for each service. 145 W Ash Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 259-3200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS RESOURCES HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, shoes, bedding, household items, and clothing for babies and newborns. Items are subject to availability. 1755 13th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Saturday 9am-noon, closed holidays Email (503) 364-9936 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES PARENTING RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource closet of baby clothing and toys for enrolled program participants. 4900 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Parents age 22 and younger Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 239-6996 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. (hats, umbrellas, gloves, scarves, and socks), kitchen supplies, laundry service, bedding/linens, personal grooming supplies/toiletries, over-the-counter medications, personal care kits, physical therapy, showers, water bill assistance, and firewood. Accepts donations of bedding and linen, clothing, and personal grooming supplies. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF BENTON COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, personal hygiene items, linens (bedding, towels, etc.), baby bundles (newborn baby supplies and clothing), kitchen supplies, and a gift closet (for when community members can't afford to purchase gifts for special occasions). 4900 NW Hwy 99 Suite 102 Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Low-income community members in need Hours: Helpline: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-1:30pm Email (541) 757-8111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST A FAMILY PLACE MCMINNVILLE CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, maternity clothing, and emergency diapers. Families can receive a 3-day supply of diapers once a month when supplies are available. 125 SE Cowls Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Families in need of diapers and clothing for children ages 0-5, or those who need maternity clothing Hours: Thursday 10am-noon Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST A FAMILY PLACE NEWBERG CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, maternity clothing, and emergency diapers. Families can receive a 3-day supply of diapers once a month when supplies are available. 600 E 6th Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Diapers and children's clothing: Families with children ages 0-5 Maternity clothing: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 10am-noon Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST A FAMILY PLACE WILLAMINA CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, maternity clothing, and emergency diapers. Families can receive a 3-day supply of diapers once a month when supplies are available. 1100 NE Oaken Hills Drive Willamina, OR 97396 Eligibility: Families with minor children ages 0-5 Hours: Thursday 10am-noon Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARLA'S MALL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift store with books, toys, household items, new hygiene products, knick-knacks, and clothing including interview or work-appropriate attire. Offers volunteer work experience and credit recovery for students working at the store. Also accepts donations of new and gently used items. 2500 E Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Thrift Store: Unrestricted Volunteers: Baker School District high school students Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 8am-11am and 1pm-4pm, closed holidays. Hours are subject to change. Summer hours: June 16th-July 17th Monday-Thursday 8:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 524-2309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER CLOTHING AND BABY SUPPLIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides maternity clothing and supplies for pregnant women and nursing mothers. Also provides baby clothing, baby blankets, sleepers, toys and children's books, as well as emergency supplies of diapers, formula, and baby food. Strollers, infant bathtubs, car seats, and pack and plays available dependent on availability. Parents are encouraged to become a client (which is free) and allows access to more services. Diapers and formula are available for emergency needs only and not provided on an ongoing basis. Mother & Child is not a clothing closet; it is a program which has supplies to assist families but also focuses on education and support. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Pregnant or parents/primary caregivers of a child aged birth to 5 years old. No eligibility requirements based on income. The client must be able to come to the Mother and Child Education Center to receive services. Diapers, clothing, and other goods cannot be mailed or delivered to clients. Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm, Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR CLOTHING AND TOYS CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers children's clothing, toys and supplies. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Open to any families that would like to either donate or take gently used baby clothing, toys, and baby equipment. Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 10 am-12pm and 1-3pm Email (541) 386-6665 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR NEXT DOOR WASCO CLOTHING AND TOYS CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers children's clothing, toys and supplies. 1113 Kelly Avenue The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Open to any families that would like to either donate or take gently used baby clothing, toys, and baby equipment. Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 10 am-12pm and 1-3pm Email (541) 296-8118 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nursery providing services for at-risk families that include therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers, home visiting, outreach and parent support groups. Resource closet available with food, children's clothing, diapers, wipes, hygiene items and cleaning supplies. 1650 SW 45th Place Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who are low-income, a single-parent, teen parent, experiencing mental health or drug/alcohol issues, victims of trauma or domestic violence, involvement with child welfare, homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 757-8068 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PHILOMATH COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry once per month. Gleaning program distributes gleaned surplus food weekly. Provides a children's clothing closet and diapers. Also offers a community garden and gardening instruction to community members. Donations and volunteers welcome. 360 S 9th Street Philomath, OR 97370 Eligibility: 300% of the Area Median Income June's Kids Kloset: Ages 0-17 Hours: Food pantry: Tuesday 4:30pm-7:30pm, Thursday 9am-noon, 3rd Saturday of the month 12:30pm-3:30pm Gleaning program distribution: Varies June's Kids Kloset: Tuesday 4:30pm-7:30pm, Thursday 9am-noon, 3rd Saturday of the month 12:30pm-3:30pm Lupe's Community Garden: Open seasonally If Philomath Schools are closed due to weather, the food pantry and June's Kids Kloset will be closed as well. Email (541) 929-2499 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND PUBLIC SCHOOLS BENSON POLYTECHNIC HIGH SCHOOL AT MARSHALL CAMPUS PTA CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a clothing closet for families with school-aged children. Donations accepted. 3905 SE 91st Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Grades K-12 Hours: Monday/Thursday 10am-1pm beginning Thursday, September 4, 2025 through Thursday, April 30, 2026 Email (503) 916-5100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT 48 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tailored bags with essential items for foster children entering care, including clothing, hair care products, and LGBTQ2S+ sensitive options. Offers a six-week program for youth aging out of foster care, featuring workshops on financial literacy, resume building, and interview preparation. 2505 SE 11th Avenue Suite 122 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Foster youth ages 7-23 Hours: Varies Email (971) 275-7416 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT LEMONADE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth in foster care can get new or nearly new outfits, accessories, and shoes, which they get to choose themselves. 1008 Lloyd Center Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 5-20 in the ODHS Child Welfare system with an open case Hours: Tuesday-Friday 10am-5pm, Saturday 10am-1pm Email (503) 395-3976 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER IN HIS HANDS CLOTHING MINISTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing depending on availability. Donations of clothing, backpacks, and school supplies accepted. 2555 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 4pm-7pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 673-5918 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHERWOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER SHARE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support for families in need. Offers the use of computers and office equipment for job search and accessing community resources. Gives referrals to parenting classes. 21920 SW Sherwood Boulevard Sherwood School District Office Sherwood, OR 97140 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm, during the school year. Summer hours by appointment only. Email (503) 825-5087 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHERWOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER FAMILY RESOURCE COORDINATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about local child care, preschool, and kindergarten options. Supplies basic resources like clothing, formula, diapers, and wipes. Offers referrals and application assistance to other resources as needed. Hosts ongoing parenting classes, early learning workshops, and an annual early learning fair. 21920 SW Sherwood Boulevard Sherwood School District Office Sherwood, OR 97140 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who live within the Sherwood School District Boundaries (do not have to have a child in school) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-3pm Email (503) 825-5087 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES SNOWCAP'S CLOTHING PROGRAM CLOTHES FOR THE WHOLE FAMILY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a clothing closet with gently used clothing. Also offers diapers. Supplies are limited and dependent on donations. 21655 SE Stark Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: -Clothing closet: Low-income households; people may visit the clothing closet once a month -Diapers: Age 2 and under Hours: Tuesday-Thursday by appointment only. Same day appointments may be available. Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST MARK'S LUTHERAN CHURCH BETHLEHEM CHILDREN’S CLOTHES CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children’s clothing (sizes preemie through teens), maternity clothing, baby supplies (diapers, wipes, pull-ups, formula, and car seats), and toys, while supplies last. Also accepts donations 5415 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Ages 0-17 for clothing; expectant parents Hours: Clothing: By appointment only Donations: Tuesday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (503) 777-1443 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SWEET POTATO'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides essential resources and support to individuals and families, with a special focus on resource families and at-risk youth. Provides clothing, diapers, wipes, toiletries, hygiene items, and other resources. 167 E Main Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Resource families may access resources as needed. Other clients must be engaged with and accompanied by a community partner. Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-3pm, Friday 1pm-7pm Email (541) 371-2224 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE CONTINGENT MY NEIGHBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects goods and services from community members to families and youth impacted by foster care. 809 N Russell Street #203 Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: -Resource families and foster children/youth -Biological families and relative providers with an open ODHS Child Welfare case Hours: No standard hours (503) 281-1801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TIGARD-TUALATIN FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works together with Family Partnership Advocates in schools to provide information and referral for services such as OHP, SNAP, WIC, housing support, furniture support, legal help, and basic needs assistance. 9550 SW Murdock Street Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-18 or pregnant individuals Hours: By appointment only (503) 603-1585 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WARRENTON FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH CHILDREN'S CLOTHING BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers gently used clothing with sizes for infants through sixth grade. Accepts donations of new car seats, gently used toys, and children's clothing. 30 NE 1st Street Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Ages 0-12 Hours: Friday, 3pm-6pm and Saturday, 10am-1pm (503) 791-7522 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WITH LOVE RESOURCE FAMILY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides new and gently used clothing, shoes, toys, books, and supplies. Delivery is available to those living within 30 minutes of the Tigard facility. Scheduled pick-ups at the Tigard facility are available for others, Monday-Friday. There are textured hair care classes for transracial resource parents. 14555 SW 74th Avenue Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Resource parents with children in foster care ages 0-6 (up until 7th birthday) and after the child's case has changed to permanency Hours: By appointment Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BABY RESOURCES BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BABY RESOURCES DALLAS EVANGELICAL CHURCH COMMUNITY CLOTHING CLOSET EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER ITEMS EXCHANGE PROGRAM FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF PRINEVILLE HOPE'S CLOSET FISH OF LEBANON BASIC NEEDS HELPING HANDS RESOURCES HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHING ASSISTANCE INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES PARENTING RESOURCES LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES LOVE INC OF BENTON COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK MARLA'S MALL MARLA'S MALL MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER CLOTHING AND BABY SUPPLIES NEXT DOOR CLOTHING AND TOYS CLOSET NEXT DOOR CLOTHING AND TOYS CLOSET OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY PHILOMATH COMMUNITY SERVICES PHILOMATH COMMUNITY SERVICES PORTLAND PUBLIC SCHOOLS PTA CLOTHING CLOSET PROJECT 48 PROJECT 48 PROJECT LEMONADE PROJECT LEMONADE ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER IN HIS HANDS CLOTHING MINISTRY SHERWOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER SHARE CENTER SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES CLOTHES FOR THE WHOLE FAMILY ST MARK'S LUTHERAN CHURCH BETHLEHEM CHILDREN’S CLOTHES CLOSET SWEET POTATO'S CLOSET SWEET POTATO'S CLOSET THE CONTINGENT MY NEIGHBOR TIGARD-TUALATIN FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER TIGARD-TUALATIN FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER WARRENTON FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH CHILDREN'S CLOTHING BANK WITH LOVE RESOURCE FAMILY SUPPORT
